title: fritillaria obliqua subsp. tuntasia
intro: Fritillaria obliqua subsp. tuntasia, also known as Tuntasia fritillaria, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the lily family (Liliaceae), native to the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau in China. It is used in traditional Chinese medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, anti-viral, and anti-bacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory infections and other diseases. In herbal preparations, Fritillaria obliqua subsp. tuntasia is often used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and infusions, which are typically consumed to promote overall health and well-being.
